flags.message: This is an early-1800s mandatory-jurisdiction case (Mutual Assurance Soc. v. Watts', 1 Wheat. 279 (1816)) — the pre-1925 category the predict scope excludes — but its corpus row is bare (no docket number, no dates, predict_excluded=0), so the exclusion predicate had nothing to classify on and the case reached a predictor. A 'petition disposition' event is anachronistic here: no discretionary petition existed to grant or deny.

The provisioned snapshot carries almost no facts: no docket entries, no docket number, no filed/argued/terminated dates, no parties beyond the caption — only the case name and one opinion-cluster link. The docket may be recoverable via the cluster (probe-recoverability's cluster-disposition path). Prediction was made from base rates plus era classification; treat it as low-information.
